definition: A system of government where people vote for leaders
example: Democracy requires free and fair elections.

definition: Laws considered collectively
example: New legislation was passed to protect workers.

definition: An organized effort to achieve a political goal
example: The election campaign lasted six months.

definition: A vote in which people decide a specific question
example: The country held a referendum on EU membership.

definition: Managing relations between countries
example: Diplomacy is preferred over military action.

definition: A system of government with many complex rules
example: The bureaucracy makes it hard to get things done.

definition: The political parties not in government
example: The opposition criticized the new policy.

definition: To form or make up something
example: These groups constitute the majority.

definition: To make people obey a law or rule
example: The police enforce traffic laws.

definition: To publicly support a cause or policy
example: She advocates for equal rights.

definition: To make an agreement official by voting
example: Parliament ratified the treaty.

definition: An official punishment or restriction against a country
example: Economic sanctions were imposed on the regime.

definition: The power of a country to govern itself
example: National sovereignty must be respected.

definition: Supported by two political parties
example: The bill had bipartisan support.

definition: Demanding obedience to authority, not allowing freedom
example: The country has an authoritarian government.

definition: Dishonest or illegal behavior by people in power
example: The report exposed government corruption.

definition: Relating to government money and spending
example: The fiscal policy aims to reduce debt.

definition: A group of parties joining together to govern
example: The two parties formed a coalition government.

definition: A change or addition to a law or document
example: The First Amendment protects free speech.

definition: A plan of action adopted by a government or organization
example: The government announced a new health policy.
